title: MSME Financial & Statutory Advisory
shortDescription: Udyam registration, statutory MSME 45-day payment rule (Section 43B(h)) compliance, subsidized credit schemes, and tax structuring.
fullDescription: Micro, Small, and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) form the backbone of the Indian industrial economy, governed by dedicated legal and financial frameworks. Anil Puneet and Associates provides regulatory advisory and statutory compliance services tailored specifically for MSME units. We guide businesses through Udyam registration, Section 43B(h) payment compliance, MSME Samadhaan delayed payment proceedings, Interest Subvention credit schemes, and government subsidy documentation.
scopeOfWork: Udyam Registration filing, classification updating, and NIC code optimization
Section 43B(h) Income Tax compliance audit to track 45-day/15-day vendor payment mandates
MSME Form I semi-annual reporting for outstanding supplier dues to MCA
MSME Samadhaan filing assistance for recovery of delayed commercial payments with statutory interest
Financial project report preparation for MSME bank loans, CGTMSE, and Mudra credit applications
Interest subvention, capital subsidy, and ZED (Zero Defect Zero Effect) certification advisory
Presumptive taxation planning under Section 44AD and 44ADA of the Income Tax Act
Working capital management and cash-flow cycle reviews for small and medium enterprises

process.title: MSME Classification Audit
process.description: Evaluating turnover and investment in plant & machinery to verify Micro, Small, or Medium status.

process.description: Analyzing accounts payable ledgers against written agreements to ensure Section 43B(h) compliance.

process.description: Filing statutory MSME returns and providing periodic compliance updates to management.

deliverables.description: Official Ministry of MSME certificate with complete NIC activity codes.

deliverables.description: Comprehensive audit schedule identifying compliant vs. overdue MSME supplier balances.

deliverables.description: Filed semi-annual return detailing outstanding dues exceeding statutory payment periods.

faqs.question: What is the impact of Section 43B(h) of the Income Tax Act on MSME payments?
faqs.answer: Section 43B(h) mandates that payments due to Micro and Small Enterprises must be made within the time specified in a written agreement (up to 45 days) or within 15 days in the absence of an agreement. Delayed payments are disallowed as expense deductions in that assessment year and taxed accordingly.

faqs.question: What are the investment and turnover limits for MSME classification?
faqs.answer: Micro: Investment <= ₹1 Crore and Turnover <= ₹5 Crores; Small: Investment <= ₹10 Crores and Turnover <= ₹50 Crores; Medium: Investment <= ₹50 Crores and Turnover <= ₹250 Crores. Both conditions must be satisfied.

faqs.question: How does the MSME Samadhaan portal assist in delayed payment recovery?
faqs.answer: Under the MSMED Act, 2006, buyers delaying payment beyond 45 days are liable to pay compound interest at three times the RBI bank rate. MSME Samadhaan enables registered units to file reference applications before the Micro and Small Enterprises Facilitation Council (MSEFC).

keyHighlights: Dedicated focus on Section 43B(h) compliance verification
Expertise in preparing CMA data and Project Reports for bank credit
End-to-end guidance on Udyam registration and statutory MSME rights
Structured vendor aging analysis for working capital control
